entries.title: **Hands-free unlocking, when approaching the vehicle;**
entries.content:
[Title] **Hands-free unlocking, when approaching the vehicle;**
[Content] With the card in access zone *3*, the vehicle will unlock. Unlocking is indicated by **one flash** of the hazard warning lights and the indicator lights.

Depending on the vehicle, the door mirrors fold in/out automatically when the vehicle is locked/unlocked ➥ [1.90](#page--1-0)

![](_page_0_Picture_5.jpeg)

entries.title: **Hands-free locking when moving away from the vehicle**
entries.content:
[Title] **Hands-free locking when moving away from the vehicle**
[Content] With the card on you, doors and luggage compartment door closed, move away from the vehicle: it will lock automatically once you have left the access zone *3*.

**Note:** the distance at which the vehicle locks depends on the surroundings.

To indicate that the vehicle has been locked, the hazard warning lights **flash twice** and then **light up** for approximately four seconds, and a beep sounds in confirmation.

![](_page_0_Picture_10.jpeg)

If the card has been within the detection area *3* for approximately 15 minutes, remote locking is disabled. To lock the vehicle, press the *5* button on the card or, depending on the vehicle, the *2* button on the handle *1*.

The vehicle cannot be remotely locked if the card is within the area *4*.

entries.title: The plot of inhibitor concentration over degree of surface coverage versus inhib...
entries.content:
[Title] The plot of inhibitor concentration over degree of surface coverage versus inhib...
[Content] The plot of inhibitor concentration over degree of surface coverage versus inhibitor concentration gives a straight line as shown in Fig. 5. The strong correlation reveals that egg shell adsorption on stainless surface in 0.5 M H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub> follow Langmuir adsorption isotherm. Figs. 6–8 show the SEM/EDX surface morphology analysis of stainless steel. Figs. 7 and 8 are the SEM/EDX images of the stainless steel specimens without and with inhibitor after weight loss experiment in sulphuric acid medium. The stainless steel surface corrosion product layer in the absence of inhibitor was porous and as a result gives no corrosion protection. With the presence of ES, corrosion damage was minimized, with an evidence of ES present on the metal surface as shown in Fig. 8.

![](_page_0_Figure_7.jpeg)

Fig. 5. Langmuir adsorption isotherm of ES.
[Keywords] EDX, ES, SEM, SO
